text: Female runners with a history of plantar fasciitis exhibit greater maximum instantaneous vertical ground reaction force load rates compared to healthy controls.
A lower medial longitudinal arch (lower arch index) is associated with a history of plantar fasciitis in female runners.
Increased passive ankle dorsiflexion range of motion is associated with a history of plantar fasciitis in female runners.
Rearfoot kinematics (peak eversion, eversion excursion, time to peak eversion, peak dorsiflexion) do not differ between female runners with and without a history of plantar fasciitis.
Calcaneal valgus during standing does not differ between female runners with and without a history of plantar fasciitis.
Reducing vertical impact loading rate (e.g., through cadence increase, softer landing, or gait retraining) may help prevent plantar fasciitis in runners with low arches.
